methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $500M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Wandelbots, a Series G robotics and manufacturing automation company, has raised $126M to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, though narrow peer clustering limits direct multiples application.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised of $126M as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement yields an implied valuation of $625M. This method anchors to observed progression patterns rather than peer multiples, which cannot be reliably established given the limited same-stage and similarly-valued comparable set in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector (0 same-stage peers, 1 adjacent-stage peer identified).

Analysis supports a valuation range of $531M to $719M, with a median of $625M. The single-method triangulation reflects methodological constraint rather than conviction; peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed recent round terms for Wandelbots, which would strengthen stage-progression calibration, and the structural fragmentation of robotics subsectors (warehouse automation, manufacturing manipulation, logistics) limiting direct peer alignment. Validation would benefit from contemporaneous transaction data within the automation and industrial robotics segments.
